metadata.title: One Software. Every Light.
metadata.description: Cross-platform RGB lighting control software. Unify all your RGB devices. No more software conflicts.

hero.description: Unify all your RGB devices. No more software conflicts. Cross-platform control for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

editor.title: Effect Editor
editor.description: Drag-and-drop visual effect editor. Create stunning lighting without coding.

gameSync.title: Game Sync
gameSync.description: Real-time game event triggers. Your lights react to in-game action.

cloudSync.title: Cloud Sync
cloudSync.description: Your configs everywhere. Seamless sync across all your devices.

plugin.title: Plugin Ecosystem
plugin.description: Infinite possibilities. Extend with community-built plugins.

cta.title: Ready to light up your setup?
cta.description: Download now and take control of every light in your room.

notFound.description: Page not found. The light may have gone out here.

contact.title: Contact

supportEmail.title: Support Email
supportEmail.description: Use email for installation issues, device compatibility requests, and account-related questions.

businessEmail.title: Business Contact
businessEmail.description: Use this address for partnerships, licensing, OEM inquiries, and commercial discussions.

portal.title: Support Portal
portal.description: If you run a help desk or ticket system, route users there for structured issue tracking.

discord.title: Discord Community
discord.description: Best for quick setup help, community discussion, and sharing configurations.

issues.title: GitHub Issues
issues.description: Use issues for reproducible bugs, feature requests, and public tracking.

panel.title: Before You Reach Out

items.support: Include your device model, operating system, and a short reproduction path for technical problems.
items.business: For partnerships or OEM requests, include company name, product scope, and expected timeline.
items.response: Community channels are fastest for general help; email is better for formal requests.

panel.missingContact: Support and business email are still unset in environment variables. Configure NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPPORT_EMAIL and NEXT_PUBLIC_BUSINESS_EMAIL before production launch.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: The questions buyers ask before they replace fragmented RGB utilities with one control layer.

items.question: Is the product free to use?
items.answer: The site can support free, paid, or hybrid pricing models. Adjust this section to match the actual licensing and packaging of your product.

items.question: Do users need to remove existing brand software first?
items.answer: Not necessarily. A practical migration path usually starts with parallel evaluation, then disables overlapping RGB services only after users confirm stable control.

items.question: How should the supported device list be maintained?
items.answer: The public devices page should be connected to a maintained compatibility catalog, release notes, or support intake flow so buyers understand what is verified.

items.question: Can developers extend the platform?
items.answer: Yes. The site structure supports APIs, plugins, integrations, and community ecosystem messaging for developer-facing products.

items.question: What does the site cover end to end?
items.answer: It covers the full conversion path: homepage, feature pages, downloads, docs, community, and legal destinations with reusable sections.

cookieConsent.title: Privacy preferences
cookieConsent.description: We use optional analytics to understand traffic and improve the product experience. These scripts load only after you allow them, and rejecting them will not affect core site features.
